The locking was a harder call internally than people probably assume, it would've been so easy to add a small "edit just for typos" button, and I kept talredning myself out of it for exactly the reason you're describing: the moment there's an exception, the self-locking behavior stops actually meaning anything. the pencil feel is genuinely nice, ink weight feels just right under the apple pencil and the dated daily page keeps me from overthinking what to write

Upvote (1)

Report

Share

3h ago

Image 19: Ishaan Rawat

Ishaan Rawat

Maker

@recep763495Thank you, that means a lot, especially the ink weight comment. Honestly that's mostly PencilKit's own pressure response, I picked one pen style and stayed out of its way rather than trying to over-engineer the feel.

The dated page doing that for you is exactly the reaction I was hoping for. A blank canvas asks "what should this be," a dated page just asks "what happened today," which is a much smaller question to actually answer. Glad it's working the way I meant it to.

Upvote

So let's go there. And then there it is. Dinner with friends 700 p.m. Carbone. It has the address. If I click on it, it actually captured the phone number as well. results.title: Chapter 2: Building A Smart Note Taking System – The AI Turn in Academic Research
results.content:
#### 2.5. Google Keep

If you’re already working within the Google ecosystem, Google Keep is a lightweight note-taking alternative worth exploring. We wouldn’t call it a full-blown research management tool like Obsidian or Notion, but it definitely stands out when it comes to speed, simplicity, and accessibility. Google Keep is especially useful for capturing ideas quickly (i.e., fleeting notes) and in a variety of formats. You can jot down text notes, record audio (which it transcribes automatically), snap a photo of a book page or slide, and even create to-do lists. It’s especially handy when you’re reading something on the go or need to capture a fleeting thought without overthinking structure. [...] While it’s not a slip-box system like what you’d build in Obsidian, Evernote can still be a solid place for organizing project-specific notes, keeping research materials accessible, and collaborating with others.

#### 2. 4. OneNote

OneNote is another good alternative note taking platform to consider especially if you’re already in the Microsoft ecosystem. OneNote is flexible, cross-platform, and good at helping you keep a sprawling research project from turning into a chaotic mess. [...] As Ahrens (2017) aptly observes, “every intellectual endeavour starts with a note” (p. 1). These are raw materials, the seeds from which ideas grow. And these seeds are not planted in front of a blank page; they’re sown during the learning process itself.

Ahrens further reminds us that “the process of writing starts much, much earlier than that blank screen and that the actual writing of the argument is the smallest part of its development” (p. 1). In other words, effective writing doesn’t begin with typing, it begins with deliberate, purposeful note-taking.

results.title: Here’s How to Start Journaling and Actually Enjoy It
results.content:
#### Freeform vs. guided journals

Intimidated by the blank page or screen? There are tons of guided journals and workbooks that encourage you to open up because they “give you permission to explore and talk about things you otherwise wouldn’t,” says Logan. Some center on particular topics or identities, like the Mindfulness Journal for Depression or the Mental Health Journal for Black Women. Others provide time or space restraints, like the popular One Line A Day memory book or this astrological journal that gives you a half page per day.And some apps like Reflectly, Dabble Me, and Grid Diary offer a mix of specific writing prompts to keep you focused. [...] Like me, you may have to experiment a bit to find the best match(es). Analog lovers may take a second to find a pen that doesn’t bleed in your favorite journal…or a favorite journal in the first place. For the digital types, you might dig apps like Daylio, Day One, or Happyfeed and how they’re with you all the time if you’re attached to your phone. Or you might be more comfortable typing on the computer, recording voice notes, or a mix of more than one.

#### Freeform vs. guided journals
